source.description: Project-provided high-school Spanish curriculum workbooks for Year 1 and Spanish II.

sources.reviewNotes: Workbook review notes flag chapters 15 and 18 for proofread attention; no obvious OCR errors were found in the focused intake pass.

source.rightsStatus: Project-provided source material; provenance documentation remains to be finalized.

spanishIIImport.tierAssignment: Year 2 entries are assigned to the Expanding 2 application tier.

sourceAlternativeSplits.note: Clear comma-separated Spanish alternatives are represented as separate active entries; poner, el garaje, and la cocina alternatives are omitted where those Spanish prompts already exist as distinct entries.

notes: Chapter is the fine-grained curriculum order; the four application tiers are derived chapter and year bands.
Learner-facing Spanish is separated from the canonical lemma and source forms.
Stem changes, required prepositions, reflexivity, irregular forms, and related notes are retained as structured grammar metadata.
Repeated Spanish prompts are merged for fair multiple-choice questions; distinct source glosses are retained in the senses array.
The former 1,500-entry testing vocabulary remains in the repository as possible future supplementary material but is no longer active.
Previously blank parts of speech were inferred from articles, infinitive forms, learner-facing glosses, and explicit review of introductory expressions.
Learner-facing interrogatives use the question category; other former expressions use phrase so question distractors can be weighted independently.
First-pass semantic tags adapt the Instituto Cervantes Nociones específicas organization to this curriculum; tags are project-authored and remain subject to editorial review.
Every entry has a year label; repeated prompts can carry multiple years while retaining the year of first introduction.
Year 1 chapters 7–9 are Expanding 1; Spanish II chapters 10–18 are Expanding 2.
The source's official Spanish-English teaching text is preserved except for quiz-only overrides used to remove misleading production prompts.
Clear comma-separated Spanish alternatives that can be quizzed independently are split into separate active entries; punctuation and grammar-variant comma entries remain combined.
